description: Address review issues: (1) Replace 2-second timestamp heuristic with device-specific ID for self-echo suppression, (2) Guard cloud save effect against unnecessary saves on project switch, (3) Fix toast timing by using per-toast timers instead of shared timer.
steps: Step 1 - Device ID self-echo suppression: Generate a unique deviceId using crypto.randomUUID() at module level. Include this deviceId in the saved payload as _lastWriter. In the realtime handler, check if payload.new.data._lastWriter === deviceId to skip self-echoes. Keep timestamp check as secondary fallback.
Step 2 - Cloud save guard on project switch: Add a skipNextSaveRef boolean that gets set to true in switchProject and cycleToProject, and checked/reset in the cloud save useEffect.
Step 3 - Per-toast timers: Replace the single shared timer approach with per-toast individual timers. When a toast is added, set a timeout for that specific toast ID. Clear timers on unmount.
acceptance_criteria: Running `npm run build` passes with no errors
A deviceId is generated at module level and included in cloud save payloads
The realtime handler checks _lastWriter === deviceId before the timestamp fallback
Switching projects does not trigger an unnecessary cloud save
Each toast gets exactly 3 seconds of display time regardless of other toasts
verification: Run `npm run build` and confirm it succeeds with no errors
findings: All three review issues addressed in src/App.jsx. Build passes. (1) deviceId generated via crypto.randomUUID() at module level, included as _lastWriter in cloud save payload, checked first in realtime handler before timestamp fallback. (2) skipNextSaveRef added and set to true in both switchProject and cycleToProject, checked and reset in the cloud save useEffect. (3) Toast timers are now per-toast using a ref map of toast ID to setTimeout handle, with cleanup on unmount. Toast IDs now use Date.now() + Math.random() to avoid collisions.
